monster.name: JasonAccordion
monster.type: humanoid (human (in life), now a trapped soul)

trait.name: Sentient Item
trait.text: Jason exists as a soul in the Ring of the Virtuoso. He has no physical body. He communicates telepathically with the wearer. The stats above represent his mental and magical capabilities, not a body.

trait.name: Spellcasting (as lv10 bard)
trait.text: spell save DC 16, spell attack +8. Slots: 4/3/3/3/2. Jason can only cast spells through the wearer (when he has control).

trait.name: Supreme Manipulator
trait.text: advantage on Deception and Persuasion checks. Creatures that speak with Jason (through the wearer) have disadvantage on saves against his charm spells.

trait.name: Progressive Possession
trait.text: see "Progressive possession" section above. Jason doesn't fight directly — he takes control of the wearer.

action.name: Psychic Wave (when a 1 comes up on the d8)
action.text: Saving throw: none (automatic) Damage: 1d4 + grip level psychic damage Area: all creatures within 15m / 50ft / 10qd of the wearer Effect: hits allies and enemies indiscriminately

action.name: Mental Conflict (when Jason forces control)
action.text: Saving throw: Wisdom DC 14-16+ (varies with grip) Failure: Jason takes control of the wearer for 1d4 rounds (combat) or 1d4 hours (out of combat) Success: the wearer resists, grip decreases by 1

action.name: Nightly Nightmares (grip 3+)
action.text: Damage: 2d6 psychic per night Effect: no benefit from long rest

monster.description: In life, Jason Accordion was a handsome man with a smile that never reached his eyes — the type who walks into a room and everyone turns to look. Warm voice, impeccable manners, always the right word at the right time. Now he is just a voice in the ring. He whispers, suggests, flatters. The tone is that of an old friend — patient, understanding, never aggressive. At first. Over time he becomes more insistent, more present, more *him* and less the wearer.
